title: Intense Crunchy French Sesame Salad
description: A cultured, brine‑wet, macrobiotic-friendly salad bursting with toasted sesame crunch and nostalgic French flair.
instructions: Heat a dry skillet over medium heat and toast 50 g white sesame seeds until fragrant, about 3 minutes; transfer to a plate to cool.
Repeat with 25 g black sesame seeds for another 2‑3 minutes until lightly browned, then set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp honey, 1 tsp grated ginger, and 1 tsp toasted sesame oil until well blended.
Roughly halve 100 g pickled cucumber into thin slices and thinly slice 80 g radish into julienne.
In a large bowl, combine 150 g mixed salad greens, sliced cucumber, radish, 50 g cooked edamame, and thinly sliced green onion.
Pour the prepared dressing over the salad mixture and toss gently until everything is evenly coated.
Sprinkle the toasted white and black sesame seeds over the top, giving the dish its signature crunch.
Let the salad rest for 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and the seeds to absorb some of the dressing.
Give the salad one final quick toss before serving, optionally garnishing with extra sesame seeds or a drizzle of brine from the pickled cucumber.
